title: Whistleblower Dave Grusch: UFOs Are Hidden From Presidents
summary: Dave Grusch discusses how certain presidents historically have not had access to UAP-related information due to 'need to know' restrictions. He explains that career officials often withhold information from Congress and the White House, citing leak concerns, unless a president has prior intelligence community experience like George H.W. Bush.

acronyms.definition: U.S. intelligence agency responsible for gathering and analyzing national security information

key_claims.claim: Certain presidents historically don't have a need to know on UAP topics unless they need to sign an executive order or make a command decision
Career senior officials who have been in government for 20-30 years deliberately withhold information from Congress and the White House because of leak concerns
George H.W. Bush likely had access to UAP information because he was CIA director before becoming president
Grusch was an executive branch employee for 14 years
